body.label: Example7.root.thm
body.name: example7_root_thm
body.claim: ∀ (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X], (∀ U : Set X, IsOpen U ↔ U = ∅ ∨ Set.Countable (Set.univ \ U)) → ¬ Set.Countable (Set.univ : Set X) → ∀ (x : ℕ → X) (L : X), Tendsto x atTop (𝓝 L) ↔ ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ N → x n = L

hypothesis.assumption: $X$ is an uncountable set equipped with the co-countable topology.
In the co-countable topology on $X$, a set $U$ is open iff $U=\emptyset$ or $X\setminus U$ is countable.
$(x_n)$ is a sequence in $X$ and $L\in X$.
Let X be an uncountable set equipped with the co-countable topology

if_proof.claim_label: If (x_n) converges to L in the co-countable topology, then there exists N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n = L.

proof_steps.assumption: (x_n) converges to L in the co-countable topology

proof_steps.value: {y : X | ∃ n : ℕ, y = x_n ∧ y ≠ L}
proof_steps.properties: the set of all sequence values different from L

deduced_from_theorem.claim: The range of a function from ℕ is countable.
deduced_from_theorem.name: countability of the range of a sequence
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied to the sequence n ↦ x_n.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: Every subset of a countable set is countable.
deduced_from_theorem.name: subsets of countable sets are countable
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied because A is a subset of the range of the sequence.

proof_steps.claim: A is countable
proof_steps.proof_method: Since A is a subset of the range of the sequence, and the range of a sequence indexed by ℕ is countable, A is countable.
proof_steps.source_claim: Since A is a subset of the range of the sequence, A is countable.

proof_steps.properties: the complement in X of the countable set A

proof_steps.name: u_x_a
proof_steps.claim: U = X \ A
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: In the co-countable topology, the complement of a countable subset is open.
deduced_from_theorem.name: definition of the co-countable topology
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used with the countable set A.

proof_steps.claim: U is open
proof_steps.proof_method: By the definition of the co-countable topology, since X \ U = A is countable, U is open.
proof_steps.source_claim: Then U is open and contains L.

proof_steps.name: u_x_a
proof_steps.claim: U = X \ A
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

proof_steps.name: a_y_x_n_y_x_n_y
proof_steps.claim: A = {y : X | exists n : Nat, y = xSeq applied to n and y != L}
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

source.text: A = {y : X | ∃ n : ℕ, y = x_n ∧ y ≠ L}

proof_steps.claim: L ∈ U
proof_steps.proof_method: By definition, L is not in A because every element of A is different from L; hence L belongs to X \ A = U.
proof_steps.source_claim: Then U is open and contains L.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If the sequence xSeq converges to L, then for every open set U containing L, there exists N such that for all n with n >= N, xSeq applied to n is in U.
deduced_from_theorem.name: definition of sequence convergence in a topological space
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied to the open neighborhood U of L.

proof_steps.claim: There exists N such that for all n with n >= N, xSeq applied to n is in U
proof_steps.proof_method: Apply convergence of (x_n) to the open neighborhood U of L.
proof_steps.source_claim: By convergence, there is N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n ∈ U.

proof_steps.proof_method: Choose N from the eventual membership statement obtained by convergence.
proof_steps.source_claim: there is N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n ∈ U
proof_steps.value: a natural number such that for all n ≥ N, x_n ∈ U

proof_steps.name: n_n_x_n_u
proof_steps.claim: For all n with n >= N, xSeq applied to n is in U
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

proof_steps.name: u_x_a
proof_steps.claim: U = X \ A
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

proof_steps.claim: For all n with n >= N, xSeq applied to n is not in A
proof_steps.proof_method: For n ≥ N, convergence gives x_n ∈ U, and membership in U = X \ A means x_n ∉ A.
proof_steps.source_claim: Since x_n ∈ U means x_n ∉ A

proof_steps.name: a_y_x_n_y_x_n_y
proof_steps.claim: A = {y : X | exists n : Nat, y = xSeq applied to n and y != L}
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

source.text: A = {y : X | ∃ n : ℕ, y = x_n ∧ y ≠ L}

proof_steps.claim: For every n, if xSeq applied to n is not equal to L then xSeq applied to n is in A
proof_steps.proof_method: This follows directly from the definition of A by taking the same index n as witness.
proof_steps.source_claim: any non-L sequence value lies in A

proof_steps.claim: For all n with n >= N, xSeq applied to n = L
proof_steps.proof_method: Fix n ≥ N. If x_n ≠ L, then x_n ∈ A by the definition of A, contradicting x_n ∉ A. Therefore x_n = L.
proof_steps.source_claim: it follows that x_n = L for all n ≥ N.

proof_steps.claim: There exists N such that for all n with n >= N, xSeq applied to n = L
proof_steps.proof_method: Use the chosen N as the witness.
proof_steps.source_claim: there exists N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n = L

if_proof.text: Assume that (x_n) converges to L. Let A := {x_n | n ∈ ℕ, x_n ≠ L}. Since A is a subset of the range of the sequence, A is countable. Put U := X \ A. Then U is open and contains L. By convergence, there is N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n ∈ U. Since x_n ∈ U means x_n ∉ A, and any non-L sequence value lies in A, it follows that x_n = L for all n ≥ N.

only_if_proof.claim_label: If there exists N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n = L, then (x_n) converges to L in the co-countable topology.

proof_steps.full_claim: There exists M such that for all n ≥ M, x_n ∈ U.
proof_steps.claim: For all n with n >= M, xSeq applied to n is in U.

proof_steps.label: Example7.root.thm.proof.root.reverse.resolved1.eventual_membership.witness_verification
proof_steps.name: example7_root_thm_proof_root_reverse_resolved1_eventual_membership_witness_verification
proof_steps.claim: For all n with n >= M, xSeq applied to n is in U.

proof_steps.name: m_n
proof_steps.claim: M = N
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If a = b, then inequalities involving a may be rewritten with b.
deduced_from_theorem.name: substitution of equality
deduced_from_theorem.description: Rewrite M as N in the assumption n ≥ M.

proof_steps.claim: n ≥ N
proof_steps.proof_method: Rewrite M = N in the assumption n ≥ M.

proof_steps.name: for_all_k_n_x_k_l
proof_steps.claim: xSeq applied to n is in U
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

source.text: For all k ≥ N, x_k = L

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If ∀ k, P k holds, then P n holds for any particular n.
deduced_from_theorem.name: universal instantiation
deduced_from_theorem.description: Apply the eventual constancy hypothesis to n.

proof_steps.claim: xSeq applied to n = L
proof_steps.proof_method: Apply the hypothesis For all k ≥ N, x_k = L to the previously proved n ≥ N.

proof_steps.name: l_u
proof_steps.claim: L ∈ U
proof_steps.proof_method: Named local obligation from unresolved claim dependency.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If a = b and b ∈ U, then a ∈ U.
deduced_from_theorem.name: substitution of equality in membership
deduced_from_theorem.description: Rewrite x_n as L in the membership statement L ∈ U.

proof_steps.claim: xSeq applied to n is in U
proof_steps.proof_method: Since x_n = L and L ∈ U, substitute equals in the membership relation.

proof_steps.text: For any n ≥ M, we have n ≥ N, hence x_n = L; since L ∈ U, it follows that x_n ∈ U.

only_if_proof.text: Assume there exists N such that for all n ≥ N, x_n = L. Let U be any open set with L ∈ U. For all n ≥ N, x_n = L ∈ U, so taking M = N proves that eventually x_n ∈ U. Hence (x_n) converges to L.

source.text:
The co-countable topology on a set $X$ is defined such that $U$ is open if $U = \emptyset$ or $X \setminus U$ is countable.
Let $X$ be an uncountable set equipped with the co-countable topology. Prove that a sequence $(x_n)$ in $X$ converges to a point $L \in X$ if and only if the sequence is "eventually constant" (i.e., there exists an integer $N$ such that for all $n \ge N$, $x_n = L$).
